title: Malaria's Mighty Match: Vaccine Victory Begins
story: Cheers erupted in Yaoundé, Cameroon as the world's first mass vaccine program against malaria kicked off on January 22, 2024. This groundbreaking effort followed successful trials of the RTS,S vaccine, which proved to be a real lifesaver. The results were impressive - for every three people vaccinated, one life was protected from this dangerous disease. It's a huge step forward in the fight against malaria, offering hope to millions.
activity_question: Where did the world's first mass vaccine program against malaria begin?
follow_up_question: Why is this vaccine program so important in the fight against malaria?

title: Spearfish's Wild Weather Rollercoaster
story: Brrr! Suddenly... ahh! In 1943, Spearfish, South Dakota experienced a jaw-dropping temperature swing. On January 22, the town shivered at a frosty -4°F. Then, in just two minutes, the temperature shot up by a whopping 49 degrees! Imagine going from bundled up to unzipping your coat that fast. This rapid warm-up set a world record for the fastest temperature change ever recorded.

title: Ancient Egypt Rises in the Big Apple

title: A Woman's Fiery Stand for Equality
story: Sparks flew when Katie Mulcahey lit a cigarette on January 22, 1908. She was swiftly arrested for breaking the brand-new Sullivan Ordinance, which banned women from smoking in public. Facing the judge, Katie boldly declared, 'I've got as much right to smoke as you have.' She refused to be silenced, adding, 'No man shall dictate to me.' Her defiant words ignited a debate about fairness and freedom.

I didn't know smoking was illegal
Can I pay the fine now?
activity_answer: I've got as much right to smoke as you have
follow_up_question: Do you think it was fair to have different smoking rules for men and women?

story: Willa Brown's dreams took flight in 1906 when she was born in Glasgow, Kentucky. She soared into history as the first African American woman to earn a pilot's license. activity_question: What happened when Fidel Castro pitched in the Winter League game?
activity_choices: He signed with a pro team
bonus_fact: Before becoming a political leader, Castro was known for his love of baseball and even tried out for a professional team.

title: Baseball Legends Join the Hall of Fame
story: Two baseball greats hit a home run in 1969! On January 22, Roy Campanella and Stan Musial were elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame. These talented players wowed fans with their skills on the field. Now, their names would be forever remembered alongside other baseball heroes in Cooperstown, New York.
activity_question: Where is the Baseball Hall of Fame located?
follow_up_question: What positions did Roy Campanella and Stan Musial play?

title: The Worm That Crawled Through Cyberspace
story: Oops! A computer program gone wild caused chaos across the internet. On January 22, 1990, Robert Tappan Morris was found guilty of releasing the Morris worm. This sneaky software spread through computers in 1988, slowing down systems and causing big headaches for tech experts. It wasnt meant to be harmful, but it taught everyone an important lesson about being careful online.
activity_question: What was Robert Tappan Morris found guilty of releasing?
follow_up_question: Why is it important to be careful when using computers and the internet?

title: Bolivia's First Indigenous President Takes Office
story: Cheers erupted as Evo Morales made history in 2006. On January 22, he became Bolivia's first indigenous president. Morales, who grew up herding llamas, now led a nation. His election marked a turning point for Bolivia's native people, who had long faced discrimination. Morales promised to fight poverty and give a voice to those often ignored.
activity_question: What did Evo Morales become in 2006?
follow_up_question: How might Morales' background help him understand the challenges faced by many Bolivians?

title: Shots of Hope: Britain's Vaccine Push
story: Needles ready! In 2001, the British government rolled up its sleeves for a big health mission. On January 22, they kicked off a campaign to get more people protected against diseases. Posters, TV ads, and doctors all worked together to spread the word about how vaccines keep us safe. It was like a superhero team-up, but for health!
activity_question: What did the British government launch in 2001?
follow_up_question: Why do you think governments run campaigns to encourage vaccinations?
